Analysis limited to local evidence within e9; broader fragmentation impact characterization across all refs e9 to e12 inferred but not separately verified.

findings: Backward packet length features (Max, Mean, Std) exhibit stable class-conditioned structural variation with high class separation across examined time windows and network conditions.
No significant drift or instability detected in backward packet length variation distributions locally.

Backward packet length features (Max, Mean, Std) and Avg Bwd Segment Size exhibit significant class-conditioned structural variation with high variance ratios and unique value differences between BENIGN and DDoS classes.
High redundancy among backward packet length features confirms consistent structural patterns rather than isolated anomalies.
Moderate shortcut-like predictive leverage detected in Bwd Packet Length Mean suggests sensitivity to attack-specific packet size manipulation or fragmentation effects.
limitations: Analysis limited to local evidence within e9; broader fragmentation impact characterization across all refs e9 to e12 inferred but not separately verified.

findings: Backward packet length features exhibit class-conditioned structural variation with high variance ratios and unique value differences between BENIGN and DDoS classes.
High redundancy between Bwd Packet Length Max and Mean suggests these features share structural dependency rather than being independently confounded.
No local evidence of confounding factors such as flow duration or application type influencing backward packet length variations was detected.
limitations: Analysis limited to local evidence within backward packet length features; broader dataset context not assessed.
